about: Motivated, passionate, and technologically diverse professional seeking a dynamic technical role where I can apply strategic networking and cybersecurity skills to organizational success. Driven to enhance network operations, protect against advanced threats, and ensure regulatory compliance across mission-critical systems.
leadership: With 16 years of experience in the Army, I have developed a leadership style centered on training subordinates, employing clear communication, and delivering mission-oriented results. I have trained personnel on network-based and non-network-based programs, led systems integration projects while maintaining security and compliance, and mentored teams to foster environments where every member's skill and experience are valued.
communication: Adept at translating complex technical concepts into clear, actionable insights for technical teams, leadership, and non-technical stakeholders. I collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to align security initiatives with organizational objectives, lead training sessions across multiple fields, present assessments to executives, and facilitate discussions that drive informed decision-making.
terminalIntro: [H3x-Resume] Ready. Scroll or use sidebar to navigate.

experience.role: Communications Architect / Telecommunications Specialist / Electronics Technician
experience.summary: MOS: 25E Electromagnetic Spectrum Manager, 94E Radio Equipment Repairer. 40–105 hours per week across multiple technical duties spanning network engineering, telecommunications, cyber operations, and electronics maintenance.
highlights: Engineered, designed, implemented, and deployed an IP-based pulse emitter radio mesh network to emulate tactical Army ground force signals, enhancing cyber blue team deception operations
With authorization, planned and executed grey box penetration testing, acquired elevated internal access, and reported findings to drive policy and security improvements
Deployed and managed SIEM solutions using CentOS, Nessus, Splunk, and downtrace sensor integration across 4,000+ systems
Provided Tier-3 network support at the Army Cyber Center Southwest Asia, coordinating regional issues, root cause analysis, and vendor liaison for mission-critical infrastructure
Collaborated on remote nodal network deployment with IPS/IDS sensors, software-defined radio, and enterprise OS virtualization for secure mission-critical connectivity
Managed Active Directory for 2,000 users including RBAC, group policies, two-factor authentication, and network security controls
Ensured compliance with host-nation RF spectrum regulations and telecommunication standards across multi-country point-to-point systems
Engineered and maintained long-distance IP-based communication systems with encryption and network security protocols
Programmed and managed 5,000+ radio and IP-based communication systems across global organizational assets
Developed and led training programs on terrestrial network configurations, system architecture, and secure operational usage
Diagnosed and repaired hardware, performed system testing and calibration, and executed lifecycle upgrades to meet evolving requirements

projects.name: H3x-Dash
projects.description: Flask-based penetration testing dashboard automating scan, enumerate, exploit, and loot workflows in a single browser interface.

projects.name: Integrated Home Lab
projects.description: Personal security lab including Kubernetes Pi cluster, Nessus vulnerability scanning, VMware vSphere, IDS/IPS, and containerized services for continuous skill development.

projects.name: Tactical Radio Mesh Deception Network
projects.description: IP-based pulse emitter radio mesh network emulating tactical Army ground force signals to support cyber blue team deception operations.

projects.description: Self-hosted Exchange email and infrastructure services supporting secure communication and information sharing.
